intro:
I couldn't find any information on the plant "Sylphia fuegi." It is possible that it is a misspelling or a lesser-known plant species. If you are referring to "Silybum marianum" (also known as Silybum or Milk Thistle), here's a paragraph about it:

Silybum marianum, commonly known as Milk Thistle, is a flowering plant in the daisy family known for its potential health benefits and medicinal properties. The plant is used to treat various liver and gallbladder disorders, as well as to support the immune system and reduce inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Milk Thistle include silymarin, a flavonoid complex that is responsible for its medicinal effects, as well as other compounds such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids. Milk Thistle is commonly prepared as a dietary supplement, tea, or tincture, and is also used in herbal medicine to support liver health and promote detoxification.
